title: Crimson Soul
description: Crimson Soul is a fast-paced action RPG developed entirely as a solo project, featuring responsive combat, RPG progression, and increasingly challenging enemies. I was responsible for designing and implementing all core gameplay systems, creating visual effects, and developing the user interface.
highlights: Created shaders and visual effects for gameplay.
Implemented combat feedback and hit effects.

hero.description: Crimson Soul is a fast-paced action game with RPG elements, developed from the ground up using Godot 4 and C#.

project.label: THE PROJECT
project.title: A project built through learning.
project.paragraph1: Crimson Soul began as a personal project to learn Godot 4 and explore how far I could take a game developed primarily with C#.
project.paragraph2: What started as a project to learn a new engine eventually became an exploration of programming, architecture, 3D modeling, mobile optimization, dynamic audio, UI, and online services.

challenge.label: THE CHALLENGE
challenge.title: A new engine. A different language.
challenge.paragraph1: Godot 4 was a completely new engine for me. Additionally, I decided to use C# as my primary programming language, even though much of the engine's ecosystem and workflow is oriented around GDScript.
challenge.paragraph2: This led me to research and build my own solutions for different systems throughout the project.

warrior.title: THE WARRIOR
warrior.paragraph: The warrior is the main visual focus of the experience. Because of this, I used a bold and contrasting color palette to ensure that the character consistently stood out during gameplay.

skulls.title: THE SKULLS
skulls.paragraph1: For the enemies, I developed a toon-inspired design with a fantastical and supernatural aesthetic.
skulls.paragraph2: The goal was to maintain a dark identity while using clean and minimalist shapes.

prototype.label: FIRST PROTOTYPE
prototype.title: The first prototype was entirely 3D.
prototype.paragraph1: The initial goal was to create a 3D experience that could run on mobile devices.
prototype.paragraph2: After completing the characters and a prototype environment, I created the first Android build.

problem.title: The Problem
problem.description: The game's performance was too low when testing it on Android.

investigation.title: The Investigation
investigation.description: I implemented a custom event system and an Object Pool to reduce unnecessary operations and better control object creation and reuse.

discovery.title: The Discovery
discovery.description: The main bottleneck was not the code architecture, but the device's ability to render a 3D experience.

strategy.label: CHANGE OF STRATEGY
strategy.title: From 3D models to 2D sprites.
strategy.paragraph1: I did not want to abandon the project or return to GDScript.
strategy.paragraph2: I used the 3D models as a foundation to create sprites from different angles, using an approach similar to games that use pre-rendered 2D characters.
strategy.paragraph3: I then adapted the existing programming to work with this new visual representation.

architecture.label: ARCHITECTURE
architecture.title: Custom systems to control the project.

eventSystem.title: EVENT SYSTEM
eventSystem.description: I developed a custom event system to decouple different game systems and reduce unnecessary dependencies.

objectPool.title: OBJECT POOL
objectPool.description: I implemented an Object Pool to reuse enemies and reduce the constant creation and destruction of objects during gameplay.

music.label: DYNAMIC MUSIC
music.title: The music progresses too.
music.paragraph1: The music was created from scratch using Waveform 13.
music.paragraph2: I divided the composition into different layers and instruments. As the player progressed, new instruments were gradually introduced into the main composition.
music.paragraph3: This allowed the music to progressively increase in intensity alongside the difficulty and progression of the player.

ui.label: UI DESIGN
ui.title: Designing an interface that belonged to the world.
ui.paragraph1: I designed the user interface in Inkscape, using the dark aesthetic and visual tones established for the game's world.
ui.paragraph2: I then brought these designs into the game and developed the different menus and interface elements.

gameplayLoop.label: GAMEPLAY LOOP
gameplayLoop.title: Combat, progression, and upgrades.
gameplayLoop.description: Once the core combat system was working, I implemented the upgrade system and its interface to complete the game's main gameplay loop.

admob.title: GOOGLE ADMOB
admob.description: I integrated the Google AdMob API from scratch to implement monetization within the application.

leaderboards.title: LEADERBOARDS
leaderboards.description: I integrated Google's leaderboard functionality to allow players to compare their results.

finalResult.label: FINAL RESULT
finalResult.title: A project that grew alongside my skills.
finalResult.description: After going through different technical and visual iterations, Crimson Soul became a project that combined programming, system design, 3D modeling, mobile optimization, audio, UI, and online services.

learnings.label: WHAT I LEARNED
learnings.title: The learning behind the project.
learnings.conclusion: One of the most important lessons I learned was that developing a game is not always about optimizing the same solution indefinitely. Sometimes, the best solution is to change the way the problem itself is approached.
